completed_tasks.name: S14: Derived Slepian bridge formulas a(g), sigma_res(g) from spectral density
S14: Evaluated residual-only CV bound — fails for g < g_bar (R^2 too high)
S14: Proved two-component CV bound: CV^2 >= (1-2/pi)(1-R^2) + c(g)^2*R^2
S14: Proved chi(3) small-gap limit: c(g) -> 0.422 as g -> 0 (Slepian linearization)
S14: Proved TP2 property of gap survival S(y,g) via pathwise coupling
S14: Computed persistence psi_0 >= 0.31 via Gaussian orthant (deterministic)
S14: Rician bound gives c(g) >= 0.49, closing Step 6 analytically
S14: Full paper rewrite — 7 pages, all references resolved, PDF compiled
S14: Grok review — confirms new ground in methodology, correctly notes not closer to full RH
S14: Pivot decision — from peak-gap statistics to de Bruijn-Newman zero dynamics
S14: Deep research on dBN constant, Jensen polynomials, PF5 failure, Grochenig equivalence

remaining_tasks.name: Compute H_t(z) for the de Bruijn-Newman family using Riemann-Siegel approximation
Implement zero dynamics ODE: dz_j/dt = -2 sum 1/(z_k - z_j)
Visualize zero trajectories in complex plane as t decreases from 0.2 toward 0
Compare spacing statistics at each t against GUE predictions
Explore: what does the GUE -> arithmetic transition look like near t = 0?

human_actions_pending.context: Revised 7-page paper with fully analytical proof

human_actions_pending.action: Send revised paper to Grok for second critique
human_actions_pending.context: Paper now has no Monte Carlo, all analytical

decisions.decision: Replaced ballot lemma with two-component Slepian bound + Gaussian orthant persistence
decisions.rationale: Ballot lemma (P(exc|y) ~ cy) was a red herring; the Slepian noise floor + Rician bound close the proof without it

decisions.decision: TP2 of gap survival proved via pathwise coupling argument
decisions.rationale: X_{y2}(t) >= X_{y1}(t) pathwise since r(t) > 0; hazard rate ordering follows from stochastic domination of conditional positions

decisions.decision: Pivot from peak-gap statistics to de Bruijn-Newman zero dynamics
decisions.rationale: Peak-gap approach is statistical (positive proportion), cannot reach RH (rigidity). dBN zero flow is visual and exploratory, connects to RMT tools we already have

decisions.decision: PF5 failure blocks naive TP approach to RH
decisions.rationale: arXiv:2602.20313 proves dBN kernel is NOT PF5. Cannot extend our TP2 result to PF_infinity for kernel. Jensen polynomial program (Prong 2) remains viable for future

decisions.decision: Prong 1 (visual exploration) chosen over Prong 2 (Jensen polynomial push)
decisions.rationale: User is explorer, wants to 'poke at visualizations and ask what if'. dBN zero dynamics is inherently visual and our platform has the RMT/viz tools

next_action: Build the de Bruijn-Newman zero flow explorer. First: compute H_t(z) = integral exp(tu^2) Phi(u) cos(zu) du using numerical quadrature. Then implement the zero dynamics ODE dz_j/dt = -2 sum 1/(z_k - z_j). Visualize zero trajectories as t decreases from 0.2 toward 0. Use existing zeta zero data (_zeros_200.npy) as initial conditions at t = 0. Track GUE statistics at each t. Key reference: Polymath15 wiki and Tao's blog posts on dBN.
context_notes: Session 14 was a marathon. Started by attacking the ballot lemma (the one remaining analytical gap in the r > 0 proof). Discovered the two-component Slepian bound which splits CV into noise floor + signal floor. Proved chi(3) limit analytically. Proved TP2 of gap survival via pathwise coupling. Closed the proof with Gaussian orthant persistence (psi_0 >= 0.31, deterministic). Revised paper to 7 pages, all analytical. Grok confirmed methodology is new but correctly noted it doesn't advance toward full RH. Pivoted to de Bruijn-Newman zero dynamics as the next exploration direction. Venv still broken; use /c/Python313/python.exe. MiKTeX at /c/Users/jvaughan/AppData/Local/Programs/MiKTeX/miktex/bin/x64/pdflatex.exe.
